Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

On the other hand, lmv_intent_getattr_async() returning ENOTSUPP to the caller will make the up layer statahead to handle it as "miss" case. If there are many such "miss", it may cause the statahead to be stopped. I do not think it is the expected behavior.

Patch Set 5:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.6/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/930316e4-4259-11e5-a52e-5254006e85c2).  Ran 11 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 5: Verified+1

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.6/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/93b4fe30-425f-11e5-af45-5254006e85c2).  Ran 13 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

"On the other hand, lmv_intent_getattr_async() returning ENOTSUPP to the caller will make the up layer statahead to handle it as "miss" case. If there are many such "miss", it may cause the statahead to be stopped. I do not think it is the expected behavior."

IMHO, if it is easy to optimize the code to make state ahead not stop, then we can do it. But if it is complicate, then better leave it in this way, because that is rare case, and do not make it too complicate. Please correct me, if I misunderstood.

Patch Set 6:

PPC64 Builder: build succesful

Patch Set 6:

> IMHO, if it is easy to optimize the code to make state ahead not stop, then we can do it. But if it is complicate, then better leave it in this way, because that is rare case, and do not make it too complicate. Please correct me, if I misunderstood.

For remote object case, the LMV will return ENOTSUPP to the statahead thread, then the sataahead thread can mark related statahead entry as "REMOTE", and then in the subsequent "miss"/"hit" statistics phase, we can skip the "REMOTE" case, then it will not affect the normal "miss"/"hit" rate.

Do you think it is complex ?

parents.subject: LU-7063 llog: fix leak of lock in llog_ost_destroy()

commit.subject: LU-6578 statahead: drop support for remote entry
commit.message:
LU-6578 statahead: drop support for remote entry

This patch dropped support for remote entry statahead, because it
needs 2 async RPCs to fetch both LOOKUP lock from parent MDT and
UPDATE lock from client MDT, which is complicated. And not
supporting remote entry statahead won't cause any issue.

* pack child fid in statahead request.
* lmv_intent_getattr_async() will compare parent and child MDT,
  if child is remote, return -ENOTSUPP.

Signed-off-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Change-Id: I8c075bab0a716f194eac3c338ffbdd37f787eff6
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/15767
Tested-by: Jenkins
